description: SNV sjövatten sheet uses a virtual pool 'BPS_V2' = B342 inlets − 15 direct consumers, then distributes it fractionally to four buildings with monthly-variable R-factors. Ontology cannot mirror the fractional pool without a fractional-subtract primitive; current approach allocates via feeds edges and accepts residuals. See feedback_bps_v2_pool_revisit memory — revisit blocked pending Snowflake coverage of all pool drain meters.

B304-52-V2-AW026 is a manually-read STRUX meter with no Snowflake equivalent. Excel caches the meter's monthly value; ontology reads zero. B304 therefore under-counts by the STRUX cached value each month (−29640 Jan-2026, −43483 Feb-2026). Per project convention, STRUX-only meters are never synthesized as Snowflake rows.

Counter went offline 2025-08-05 at value 335549.97 and resumed with a device swap on 2025-11-30 at value 364454.09. Combined pattern covered by 2-segment split :d.A | :d.B via rolling_sum. The ~4-month gap is flat-carried; if B334 has upstream data or siblings that capture the physical flow, an interpolate patch could recover the integral. Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference period so no facit impact.

Device swap 2025-02-17 (last value 758607.50 → 0). Now ingested as B342.V2_VM91_V (intagsmätare 2 in the SNV.SJOVATTEN_INTAKE virtual). Handled by 2-segment :d.A | :d.B split via rolling_sum (canonical _classify_boundary detects as reset). Outside Jan/Feb 2026 reference period. The 2026-01/02 building intake totals match Excel within 0.07 % (Jan) / 1.8 % (Feb).
Counter went offline 2026-02-20 at value 9471341.0. Inside Feb 2026 reference period — affects last 8 days of the month.
Counter went offline 2025-10-18 at value 6.60 (essentially zero before offline too). Outside Jan/Feb 2026.
Counter went offline 2025-05-19 at value 4840979.0. Outside Jan/Feb 2026.

Seawater cooling seasonal pattern. Active Jun-Oct (~40-90 L/day summer peak), minimal Nov-May (mostly flat days with occasional 1-2 L/day). Not a data-quality issue — reflects physical use of seawater cooling during warm months.

Inverse seasonal pattern. Active Dec-May (~50-115k L/month), zero Jun-Nov (6-month dormancy). Likely a winter-use circuit (possibly heat-recovery or free-cooling dependent on seawater temperature). Not a data-quality issue. The 'spike' visible on 2026-02-28 is simply the normal daily rate during its active season.

B301 = 0.09 × BPS_V2 in Excel (sheet-level fractional residual pool). See ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool for the pool mechanics; revisit is blocked pending fractional-subtract primitive and complete Snowflake coverage of the BPS drain meters.
B302 = 0.18 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B303 = 0.18 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B307 = 0.46 × BPS_V2 share (largest share). Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.
B344 = 0.09 × BPS_V2 share. Same pool split as B301; see ann-snv-sv-bps-v2-pool.

BKringlan is an external-consumer (Telge Nät) metered via TE-52-V2-GF4:1, a manually-read STRUX meter with no BMS data. Excel shows cached STRUX values; ontology reports zero. Per project convention, STRUX-only meters are not synthesized into Snowflake.
BScania is an external-consumer (Telge Nät / Scania) metered via TE-52-V2-SCANIA, a manually-read STRUX meter with no BMS data. Same convention as BKringlan.

Reference formula doc sheet 'SJÖVATTEN V2' exists but has no populated formulas (header only). Current workstream ontology predates the doc and continues to be authoritative. Re-check when the doc is populated.
B308.V2_VM21-V flat at zero for the full reference period. Inactive sub-meter. Not used in building formulas; no impact.

B339.V2_GF3_4 flat 55 days ending 2025-10-19. Large-magnitude meter (median 2374 L/day). Consistent with the seasonal V9 sjövatten pattern at B339 (active Dec–May, dormant Jun–Nov). Accepted seasonal behaviour.

Campus aggregator for SNV sjövatten lake intake. Feeds-in from 2 intagsmätare at B342: V2_VM90_V (387 821 m³ Jan / 373 533 Feb) and V2_VM91_V (96 801 / 137 481, with a 2025-02-17 register-reset swap handled via rolling_sum). Sum matches Excel Σ buildings exactly (Excel design — Σ buildings is built to equal intake). Conservation panel residual ~40 % = STRUX-only meters BMS doesn't see (Kringlan, Scania, B304) plus BPS_V2 fractional pool consumption that the ontology can't allocate without a fractional-subtract primitive. The ~40 % residual is the STRUX coverage gap, not real losses.

Snowflake meter B342.V2_VM92_V exists (271 days back to 2025-06-03, ~72 k m³ Jan delta) but is NOT in Total SNV intagsmätare list nor in any Excel formula. Likely a check meter or partial-year addition; possibly a third intake added mid-year that AZ accounting hasn't yet integrated. Not ingested into the ontology. Revisit if AZ accounting recognizes it.
